description: A classification of governance events by their function in the authorization process.

namedIndividuals.description: A data-sharing or data-use agreement becomes legally effective through execution by all required parties.
namedIndividuals.name: Agreement Executed

namedIndividuals.description: A governance boundary issues a positive authorization decision.
namedIndividuals.name: Approval Issued

namedIndividuals.description: A governance issue is escalated to a higher authority or broader audience.
namedIndividuals.name: Escalation

namedIndividuals.description: An analytic product answering a stakeholder question is delivered.
namedIndividuals.name: Output Delivered

namedIndividuals.description: Data access has been technically provisioned (accounts created, data transferred, query access granted).
namedIndividuals.name: Provisioning Completed

namedIndividuals.description: An institutional actor formally initiates a governance authorization process.
namedIndividuals.name: Request Submitted

namedIndividuals.description: A governance boundary reviews a request or artifact without issuing a final decision.
namedIndividuals.name: Review

namedIndividuals.description: A partner formally or informally withdraws from a governance process.
namedIndividuals.name: Withdrawal

title: Governance Event Type

@id.description: An Internationalized Resource Identifier (IRI). May be a full IRI or a compact IRI (CURIE) resolved by the JSON-LD context.
@id.title: IRI
